sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes
Requested: Treasury withdrawal to fund Tweag by Modus Create's delivery of three Cardano core infrastructure work packages over 2026-2027.
Recipient: Tweag by Modus Create, administered through Intersect/Cardano Development Holdings treasury smart contract arrangements

Deliverables: Peras v1 production cryptography, KillSwitch, mainnet readiness, support and maintenance, History Expiry partial-history nodes to reduce SPO storage costs, Conformance Testing framework extended for Peras and Leios, Open-source deliverables publicly tracked, Ready-for-activation deliverables including merged code, reproducible releases, runbooks, benchmarks, and governance-action packages where governance activation is required
Deadline/expiry: 2026-2027 delivery period; exact milestone dates not stated in document

Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal requests ₳18,263,496.00, stated as USD $4,565,874.00, to fund three work packages across core Cardano infrastructure in 2026-2027.

Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, high materiality): The work packages are Peras v1, History Expiry, and Conformance Testing for Peras and Leios, and the proposer says they are interdependent as a single delivery pipeline.
Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, high materiality): Peras is claimed to improve finality to about two minutes versus about twelve minutes today.

Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, high materiality): History Expiry is claimed to reduce SPO storage costs by enabling partial-history nodes, with the document asserting Leios could otherwise drive disk usage to about 1 GB per hour at 100-1000 TPS.

Claim (economic, proposer_asserted, high materiality): The budget is said to be based on an average market rate of $176 per hour for senior Cardano infrastructure engineers and an ADA/USD conversion rate of 0.25 based on a five-year average.

Claim (governance, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): Intersect is requested to serve as proposal, contract, audit, and delivery assurance administrator using milestone-based fixed-price contracts where scope is well-defined.
missing: Independent evidence for: The work packages are Peras v1, History Expiry, and Conformance Testing for Peras and Leios, and the proposer says they are interdependent as a single delivery pipeline.
Independent evidence for: Peras is claimed to improve finality to about two minutes versus about twelve minutes today.

Independent evidence for: History Expiry is claimed to reduce SPO storage costs by enabling partial-history nodes, with the document asserting Leios could otherwise drive disk usage to about 1 GB per hour at 100-1000 TPS.

Independent evidence for: The budget is said to be based on an average market rate of $176 per hour for senior Cardano infrastructure engineers and an ADA/USD conversion rate of 0.25 based on a five-year average.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Treasury analysis
findings: Six-month treasury flow regime: unknown (stale snapshot)
sections.conclusion: Treasury votes require a higher bar because they consume shared ADA and create precedent.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "The proposal requests ₳18,263,496.00, stated as USD $4,565,874.00, to fund three work packages across core Cardano infrastructure in 2026-2027." and clears the evidence gates.

Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "The work packages are Peras v1, History Expiry, and Conformance Testing for Peras and Leios, and the proposer says they are interdependent as a single delivery pipeline." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: a treasury action without a complete deep-research dossier cannot be voted directionally without pretending certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.
